# Trigonometry shadow problems

## Short Teaching Summary
Trigonometry shadow problems: Trigonometry can solve problems involving the height of an object using the relationship between the angle of elevation of the sun and the length of its shadow. Key detail: In a shadow problem, the height of an object can be found by setting up equations based on two different angles of elevation. Map connection: this idea links by example_of to nearby concepts. Pause point: say the main idea in one short phrase before moving on.

0.35
